chapter_context.summary: Contains Jeremiah's famous letter to the exiles in Babylon, advising them to build houses, pray for their city, and promising restoration after seventy years.

outline.text: Letter to Exiles: Build, Plant, and Seek Peace of Babylon
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Letter to Exiles: Build, Plant, and Seek Peace of Babylon (29:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Promises of Seventy Years and Hope: 'A future and a hope'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Promises of Seventy Years and Hope: 'A future and a hope' (29:10-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Terrible Fate of Remaining Kings and False Prophets
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Terrible Fate of Remaining Kings and False Prophets (29:15-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Blasphemous Letter of Shemaiah and His Judgment
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Blasphemous Letter of Shemaiah and His Judgment (29:24-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Conflict and Exile
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of conflict and exile, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 29, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
